regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ correlated pair (as of 2026-07-15 ~12:27 UTC)

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOVE READ
Both indices are firmly above their 200-day (ES +8.4%, NQ +13.2%) and posted positive weekly returns (ES +0.91%, NQ +1.43%), so the medium-term uptrend context is intact. On the day, moves are small and constructive: ES +0.08%, NQ +0.34%. Notably, both ES (7597.25) and NQ (29891) are trading BELOW their session VWAPs (7603.81 and 29971.26 respectively) — price is holding near-highs but sellers are defending the intraday average, which is a modest short-term caution flag rather than a trend reversal. Likely catalysts: (a) geopolitics is the dominant tape driver — repeated headlines on U.S.-Iran escalation, a tanker attack in the Strait of Hormuz, oil back to $87, and a looming Strait blockade. This is a risk-off/energy-inflation cross-current that has NOT yet broken the equity uptrend but caps upside enthusiasm. (b) A soft ADP weekly jobs miss (labor-market-loses-steam narrative) is mildly dovish for rates but ambiguous for risk. (c) Bank earnings are rolling in with CPI in focus per the ET live blog, and IBM slid — idiosyncratic single-name weakness, not index-wide. Net read: grind-higher trend with a headline-driven ceiling; the tape is orderly, not euphoric.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ (WHERE volume traded — NOT order-flow delta; no buy/sell aggression is knowable from this)
ES: POC 7590.82, value area 7565.08–7602.52, dense high-volume nodes clustered 7588–7598. Price (7597.25) is sitting AT the top of the value area / just above POC — this is the accumulation shelf. The line in the sand for ES longs is value-area-low ~7565; below that, acceptance shifts lower toward prior-day low 7531.5.
NQ: POC 29833.73, value area 29480.69–30056.71, HVNs stacked 29778–29871. NQ (29891) is trading just above its POC and mid-value-area. The accumulation zone is 29778–29871; the structural line in the sand is value-area-low ~29480 (which also sits just above prior-day low 29303.5). Holding above POC keeps the constructive bias; losing 29480 signals distribution and opens the prior-week-low complex. CAVEAT: this is volume-at-price only — it tells us where trade concentrated, not whether buyers or sellers were the aggressors. True delta requires a CME order-flow feed.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME
Regime = BREADTH. Contemporaneous correlation is high (0.894) and the best lead-lag corr is a weak -0.079 with leader = NONE — there is NO clean lead-lag edge; the two are essentially moving together at lag 0. Divergence flag is FALSE. Implication: when ES and NQ agree, the directional signal is more DURABLE and can be trusted more — but 'more durable' applies to direction, not to any timing edge. The 20-day return spread is slightly NQ-negative (-0.00125) with a positive 1d spread (+0.00259), so no meaningful concentration-vs-rotation tell. Practical takeaway: trust the shared direction, but there's no leader to front-run — size normally on trend continuation, don't overstay on divergence hopes.

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS
- FOMC 2026-07-29 (14 days out, maintained seed): begin reducing size / widening stops as we approach the window.
- PCE 2026-07-31 (16 days, cadence ESTIMATE): 8:30 ET, reduce size into the print.
- ISM Manufacturing 2026-08-03 (19 days, cadence ESTIMATE): reduce size into 10:00 ET.
All are >2 weeks out, so no immediate sizing constraint today, but the FOMC window is the first hard date to respect. Note CPI is referenced in today's news flow as an imminent near-term catalyst — treat any CPI print as a today/tomorrow risk event even though it's not in the seeded calendar. Non-verified cadence dates should be confirmed against official releases.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS
Meta-regime: a durable BREADTH uptrend (both well above 200DMA, both green on the week, correlation ~0.89) running into a geopolitical headline ceiling (Iran/Hormuz/oil-$87) and near-term event risk (CPI/earnings). Structure favors longs above POC, but price under VWAP and the energy/geopolitics cross-current keep this from being a high-conviction session.
Bias: LONG (long-the-dip / trend-continuation), ~55%, invalidated on a sustained NQ break below the value-area-low ~29480 (ES analog ~7565). Above POC (NQ 29834 / ES 7591) the constructive case holds; between VWAP and POC is the chop zone where I want smaller size. 
CONFIDENCE GRADING: Moderate (55). Lead-lag is CLEAN in the sense that it's cleanly BREADTH (no divergence), which supports trusting direction — but news is MIXED (bullish trend + soft-jobs dovishness vs. genuinely risk-negative geopolitics and oil), and price is under VWAP. Clean regime + muddy news = I am deliberately capping confidence in the mid-50s rather than manufacturing certainty.
HUMILITY NOTE: This briefing is INTELLIGENCE, not a validated edge. The scorecard is n=12 (hit-rate 0.833 but regime accuracy just 0.276) and explicitly CALIBRATING — needs ~30-60 samples before any of these numbers mean anything. Also note the stated infrastructure limitation: ES/NQ is research only, no execution path here (CME futures; OANDA is forex-only). Treat every level as a reference, not a trade instruction.
